message:
fix(time): prevent spurious timeout expiry from wall-clock fallback in manual-time contexts

TimeoutFuture and the service timeout layer now only register wakers
with the underlying Sleep future when the sleep has a custom time getter
or an ambient timer driver is available in the current Cx. Previously,
after a poll_with_time check determined the timeout had not yet elapsed,
the code unconditionally polled the Sleep to register a waker. Since
Sleep without a custom time getter falls back to wall_now(), this could
cause the Sleep to fire based on real wall-clock time rather than the
virtual/manual time source used by poll_with_time, leading to spurious
timeout expiry in test suites that advance time manually.

Sleep gains a has_custom_time_getter() const method to allow callers to
check whether the sleep uses an injected time source.

Two timeout_future tests updated to use with_time_getter and explicit
set_current_time(0) initialization to ensure they operate in the
virtual-time domain and are not affected by the wall-clock state left
by other tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
